title: Lori Stein
content:
People.ContactEmail lstein@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
With in-depth knowledge of the complex Canadian securities regulatory landscape and digital asset ecosystem, Lori helps clients maximize opportunities and respond to emerging regulatory trends.
Lori Stein is co-leader of the Fintech Group and a member of the Securities Regulatory and Investment Products, Financial Services and Technology groups of our Business Law Department in Toronto. She is ranked by Chambers FinTech as a Band 1 lawyer and is a frequent speaker and thought leadership contributor on the regulation of digital assets and blockchain technology in Canada. She advises Fintech businesses, portfolio managers, dealers, investment fund managers and institutional investors on how to manage legal and regulatory risks associated with crypto assets and blockchain infrastructure, and more generally on Canadian securities regulation. She regularly engages with the Canadian Securities Administrators on behalf of crypto asset trading platforms and other Fintechs.
Lori helps clients obtain regulatory approval for innovative business strategies through applications for registration and exemptive relief under securities and derivatives regulation, and advises on day-to-day compliance issues, investigations, regulatory audits and strategic transactions.
Clients appreciate Lori’s ability to bring clarity to the complexities of the Fintech industry. At a time when innovative technologies in the digital asset ecosystem are rapidly outpacing the existing rules and regulatory framework, Lori is at the forefront of these discussions and has a thorough understanding of the relevant key legal issues.
Lori has also previously spent several years in-house at a major hedge fund and this unique, client-side perspective allows her to deliver practical advice for clients with an understanding of their internal realities.
Lori earned her J.D. from the University of Toronto and was called to the Ontario Bar in 2002. She successfully completed a Partners, Directors and Officers Exam from the Canadian Securities Institute (CSI) and holds a Canadian Securities Course designation from the CSI.

#### Representative Experience:
- Tetra Trust Company on the launch of CADD, the first Canadian dollar fiat-backed stablecoin to be issued by a regulated financial institution (May 2026)
- RBC Capital Markets, TD Securities, the Bank of Canada and Export Development Canada on Project Samara: Canada’s first tokenized bond issuance using distributed ledger technology (March 2026).
- Polygon Labs on the acquisition of Sequence, a developer of blockchain-based software and infrastructure (January 2026)
- Tensor Labs Inc., a Toronto-based digital asset software developer, in the acquisition by Coinbase Global, Inc. (Dec 2025)
- 21 Technologies Inc. and its subsidiaries COB Ledn, in the strategic investment by Tether, the El Salvador-based issuer of USDT (Nov 2025)
- Payward Canada Inc. (COB Kraken) in its registration as a restricted dealer with the Canadian Securities Administrators (March 2025)
- Ether Capital Corp. in its conversion into Purpose Ether Staking Corp. ETF (June 2024)
- Coinbase Canada Inc., in its registration as a restricted dealer with the Canadian Securities Administrators (April 2024)
- Volatility Labs Inc. DBA Fig Investments, in its sandbox exemptive relief from dealer registration from the Alberta and BC Securities Commissions (Sept 2023)
- Canadian and foreign crypto asset trading platforms (including exchanges and OTC desks). Lori has advised numerous platforms on the following matters: Securities dealer registration and marketplace regulation Responses to inquiries and investigations of the CSA Legal opinions and memoranda re: regulatory risk associated with their products and services Regulatory engagement strategies (e.g. applications for registration and exemptive relief, global strategies) Money services business (MSB) registration and compliance under AML/ATF laws Negotiations with crypto asset custodians and other service providers
- Securities dealer registration and marketplace regulation
- Responses to inquiries and investigations of the CSA
- Legal opinions and memoranda re: regulatory risk associated with their products and services
- Regulatory engagement strategies (e.g. applications for registration and exemptive relief, global strategies)
- Money services business (MSB) registration and compliance under AML/ATF laws
- Negotiations with crypto asset custodians and other service providers

### Stablecoin Act (Canada) - First Impressions
This presentation is provided for on-site viewing only. Read about Scope and Jurisdiction, Interaction with other Regulatory Regimes, Redemptions, Reserves, Reporting, Application Process, and Bank of Canada Supervision.
